mechanism_narrative: Low and stable inflation, as indicated by CPIAUCSL, provides a favorable environment for corporate earnings and equity valuations by reducing uncertainty and maintaining consumer purchasing power, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: If CPIAUCSL remains low and stable (e.g., annual growth below 2.5%), but S&P 500 20-day returns are consistently negative or flat (average return < 0.1%) over any 3-year period.

mechanism_narrative: A significant increase in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) above a certain threshold signals an economic downturn, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns as corporate profits decline due to reduced consumer spending and increased labor costs.
failure_condition: If UNRATE rises by more than 0.5 percentage points over 3 months, but S&P 500 60-day returns are positive in more than 50% of such triggered instances over a 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Strong retail sales (RSAFS) combined with robust housing starts (HOUST) indicate broad-based consumer and investment demand, which drives corporate revenues and profits across multiple sectors, resulting in positive S&P 500 forward returns.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS 3-month average growth > 0.5% AND HOUST 3-month average growth > 5%
failure_condition: If both RSAFS and HOUST show strong growth as per the trigger, but S&P 500 20-day returns are consistently negative (average return < 0%) over a 2-year period of such conditions.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of monetary tightening, rising FEDFUNDS rates increase the cost of capital for businesses and reduce liquidity in financial markets, negatively impacting equity valuations and leading to lower S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: If FEDFUNDS rises by more than 0.25 percentage points in a month during a tightening cycle, but S&P 500 60-day returns are positive in more than 60% of such instances over a 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating GDP growth (GDPC1) indicates a strong and expanding economy, which translates into higher corporate earnings expectations and increased investor confidence, thus driving positive S&P 500 forward returns.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter annualized growth rate accelerates for 2 consecutive quarters
failure_condition: If GDPC1 growth accelerates for two consecutive quarters, but S&P 500 60-day returns are negative or flat (average return < 0.1%) in more than 40% of such periods over a 10-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained job creation (PAYEMS) reflects a healthy labor market and strong consumer confidence, which supports consumer spending and corporate profits, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns due to increased economic activity.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS reports > 100k new jobs for 3 consecutive months
failure_condition: If PAYEMS consistently shows strong job growth as per the trigger, but S&P 500 5-day returns are negative in more than 50% of triggered instances over a 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production (INDPRO) signals a contraction in manufacturing and mining output, indicating weakening economic activity and corporate earnings, which leads to negative S&P 500 forward returns as investor sentiment sours.
failure_condition: If INDPRO declines for two consecutive months, but S&P 500 20-day returns are positive in more than 40% of such periods over a 7-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of rising inflation (CPIAUCSL) and low unemployment (UNRATE) indicates an overheating economy, prompting the Federal Reserve to tighten monetary policy (raise FEDFUNDS), which negatively impacts equity valuations and S&P 500 forward returns.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL annual growth > 3% AND UNRATE < 4.5% AND FEDFUNDS 3-month change > 0.25%
failure_condition: If CPIAUCSL annual growth exceeds 3%, UNRATE is below 4.5%, and FEDFUNDS shows a 3-month increase > 0.25%, but S&P 500 60-day returns are consistently positive (average return > 0.5%) over a 3-year period of such conditions.
